And as of about four weeks ago, apparently, like a large portion of the other communities out here have dropped off and are no longer participating in over the phone market surveys.

Um And I finally got an answer from one of those people and I was like, I, you probably don't know what happened, but what are you using now if you guys are not calling other properties to get these numbers?

So uh the data gets pushed through live in the morning.

And essentially we're giving you a live data feed to see where each of your units are positioned within each offset.
I love that.
So there's no need for manual call arounds or data entry.
Um We can essentially track anybody.
Everything is 100% public data.
So as long as the property has just a website with online leasing turned on, we can track um all the way down to the individual unit.

We do, we plan individual unit and uh you brought up something interesting or uh in the beginning where uh you said it seems like people aren't kind of doing the call arounds or giving out the information anymore.
So I work with probably five out of the, the top 10 largest property management companies and all advise their teams as of this year to stop sharing that information.

Um And it's all, it's like a trickle down effect, real large ones started it and then you start to move to like the mid market companies that I still work with because I'm on these calls all day, every day and everyone's relaying the same thing.
They're like, we can't manually like call around or get this, this and that.
So now everyone is looking to move towards an automated process.
So
yeah, even getting an answer from most of these properties just to for them to pick up the phone at all has been more challenging in the last few weeks.
